title: Fix 11 test failures from merge integration
description: After merging 3 parallel builder branches, 11 tests fail due to API mismatches. Root causes: (1) id.test.ts passes arrays but generateId expects Set<string> — 7 failures, (2) tpl.test.ts expects different pour/status behavior — 3 failures, (3) init.test.ts expects idempotent init but impl rejects it — 1 failure. Fix either tests or implementations to be consistent. 100/111 tests pass.
closeReason: All implementations merged to main. 111 tests pass, lint clean, typecheck clean.

title: Accept --desc as alias for --description in create and update
description: The --desc flag was silently ignored. Both create.ts and update.ts now accept --desc as a shorthand for --description.
closeReason: Fixed: both --desc and --description now accepted in create and update commands

title: Adopt Commander for CLI parsing
description: Replace the manual process.argv parsing (switch statement in src/index.ts) with Commander. Benefits: structured arg/option definitions, auto-generated help with argument types and choices, built-in version display, .addHelpText() for detailed per-command docs. Each command in src/commands/ should export a register function that takes a Commander program instance. Business logic stays untouched — only the parsing/routing layer changes.

title: Adopt chalk for output formatting
description: Replace raw ANSI escape codes in src/output.ts with chalk. Currently using manual TTY/NO_COLOR detection and c.green/c.red/c.bold/c.dim helper functions. Chalk handles terminal capability detection automatically. Migrate: c.green → chalk.green, c.red → chalk.red, etc. Preserve the outputJson/printSuccess/printError/printIssueOneLine/printIssueFull helpers but use chalk underneath. Success indicator should be ✔ (green), errors should use 'Error:' prefix (red).
closeReason: Migrated output.ts from manual ANSI escape codes to chalk. Updated printSuccess (✔ green), printError (Error: red), and all inline color calls in stats.ts, dep.ts, tpl.ts, doctor.ts. All quality gates pass.

title: Use process.exitCode instead of process.exit()
description: The main catch block in src/index.ts uses process.exit(1) which kills the event loop immediately. Switch to process.exitCode = 1 for better testability — allows the event loop to drain so bun test can capture output cleanly. The JSON error shape { success: false, command, error } is already good. Ensure all commands surface errors consistently: console.error() with chalk.red in human mode, structured JSON in --json mode.
closeReason: Replaced process.exit(1) with process.exitCode = 1 in src/index.ts catch block; added chalk.red for human-mode errors. All quality gates passed.

title: Build: Commander CLI refactor implementation
description: Implement Commander.js adoption across all 16 command files and index.ts. Install commander dep, add register() exports, rewrite routing. Subtask of seeds-afd6.

title: Visual branding: forest palette, status icons, message formats
description: Apply os-eco branding standards to seeds CLI visual output. Changes needed in src/output.ts and src/commands/{doctor,stats,tpl,dep}.ts. (1) Forest palette: define brand=chalk.rgb(124,179,66), accent=chalk.rgb(255,183,77), muted=chalk.rgb(120,120,110) in output.ts. (2) Status icon set D: replace ○/◐/● with green '-'/cyan '>'/dim 'x', add yellow '\!' for blocked. (3) Message formats: success uses brand color for ✓ and message text (not generic green), IDs in accent color. Error uses red ✗ prefix (not 'Error:'). Warning uses yellow \! prefix. (4) ID highlighting: issue IDs in accent amber everywhere. (5) doctor.ts: update ✓/⚠/✗ icons and colors to match spec. (6) All inline chalk in stats/tpl/dep: use muted for secondary info, accent for IDs. See branding/visual-spec.md and branding/checklists.md for exact spec.
closeReason: Visual branding implemented: forest palette (brand/accent/muted), status icon set D (-/>/ x/!), printWarning, accent IDs in output.ts, doctor/stats/tpl/dep commands updated. All quality gates passed.

title: CLI standards: help screen, global flags, version, upgrade command
description: Implement remaining CLI standards from branding/cli-standards.md. Changes in src/index.ts, src/commands/sync.ts, new src/commands/upgrade.ts. (1) Help screen style A: custom Commander help with brand-colored header line 'seeds v0.2.1 — Git-native issue tracking', green command names, dim args, aligned columns. (2) Version flag: change -V to -v. (3) --version --json: output {name,version,runtime,platform} envelope. (4) Global flags: --quiet/-q (suppress non-error output), --verbose (extra diagnostics), --timing (show execution time on stderr as dim text). (5) --dry-run on sd sync. (6) Typo suggestions for unknown commands (edit distance 'did you mean?'). (7) Shell completions (--completions <shell> for bash/zsh/fish). (8) New sd upgrade command: check npm registry for latest @os-eco/seeds-cli, compare with local VERSION, install via bun install -g, support --check (dry check) and --json output. See branding/cli-standards.md, branding/checklists.md, and branding/roadmap.md for full spec.
closeReason: Phase 1 CLI standards merged: -v flag, --version --json, --quiet, --verbose, --dry-run on sync, sd upgrade command. 171 tests pass. Phase 2 (help screen, typo suggestions, completions, timing) tracked in seeds-9cb0.

title: Add --root flag to sd create for writing to project root .seeds/
description: When agents run in git worktrees, sd create writes to the worktree .seeds/ directory. If that branch is never merged (e.g. lead agents), those issues are lost. Add a --root flag that writes directly to the project root .seeds/ instead of the worktree copy, so issues survive worktree cleanup regardless of merge status.
closeReason: Already solved by resolveWorktreeRoot() in config.ts — findSeedsDir() auto-resolves worktree paths to main repo .seeds/ when main repo has config.yaml. No --root flag needed.

title: Add sd unblock command to clear resolved blockers
description: There is no way to remove a blocker from an issue via the CLI. When a blocking issue is closed, the blocked issue's blockedBy array retains the stale reference. The only way to clear it today is manually editing issues.jsonl — which defeats the purpose of a CLI tool. Add: (1) sd unblock <id> --from <blocker-id> — removes a specific blocker from the issue's blockedBy array, (2) sd unblock <id> --all — clears all resolved (closed) blockers from the issue, (3) Optionally, sd ready could auto-clear closed blockers when computing the ready set so stale blocks don't accumulate. Also consider the inverse: sd block <id> --by <blocker-id> to add a blocker after creation. Currently blockers can only be set at create time or by editing JSONL directly.
closeReason: Implemented sd unblock and sd block commands. unblock supports --from (specific blocker) and --all (clear closed blockers). block supports --by. Both maintain bidirectional blockedBy/blocks consistency. 211 tests pass.

title: CI: extract changelog notes for GitHub releases
description: Update publish.yml to extract release notes from CHANGELOG.md instead of using --generate-notes. Match the pattern from overstory: awk-extract the version section, write to temp file, use --notes-file with fallback to --generate-notes if no entry found.
closeReason: Added changelog extraction step to publish.yml matching overstory pattern.
